title: Changelog generator: initial implementation + templates (public/internal filtering)
preview: Summary: Introduces the initial version of the changelog generator used to produce customer-visible model release notes (Console + docs) and internal audit-focused notes from model metadata change events. This adds (1) an event ingestion layer (with idempotency), (2) category mapping + severity normalization, (3) a ...
text: Summary: Introduces the initial version of the changelog generator used to produce customer-visible model release notes (Console + docs) and internal audit-focused notes from model metadata change events. This adds (1) an event ingestion layer (with idempotency), (2) category mapping + severity normalization, (3) a templating system aligned to the customer release notes taxonomy/style guide, and (4) strict public vs internal field filtering/redaction. Motivation: Today model/version changes are visible inconsistently across model registry, runtime config, and ad-hoc docs. Support escalations (e.g., SUP-28411) show we need a single, consistent change artifact generated from the same events that drive rollouts. This PR is the first end-to-end piece: given a stream of ModelMetadataChanged/ModelVersionPromoted events, produce deterministic changelog entries and expose them via an internal...
links: ADR-0143 Changelog generation from events vs git tags
ENG-9823 Changelog generator service internal API
ENG-9825 Public vs internal fields redaction rules
SUP-28411 Release notes missing for model version promotion

title: Console API: model release notes feed + per-model change history endpoints
preview: Summary This PR adds the Console backend endpoints needed by the new “Release notes” feed and per-model “Change history” UI. It exposes a consistent, cursor-paginated API over the changelog generator artifacts/events, with filtering by deployment mode (Hosted/Dedicated/Private), region, category, severity, model, an...
text: Summary This PR adds the Console backend endpoints needed by the new “Release notes” feed and per-model “Change history” UI. It exposes a consistent, cursor-paginated API over the changelog generator artifacts/events, with filtering by deployment mode (Hosted/Dedicated/Private), region, category, severity, model, and time window. It also enforces RBAC so internal-only fields never leak to standard Console roles (aligns with ENG-9825 + prior hotfix learnings). Motivation Customers/support have asked for an authoritative per-model history to explain output drift and to correlate rollouts with regressions (SUP-28429). Today model/version changes are visible in scattered places (registry, docs, release process), and there’s no single feed in Console with correct redaction. These endpoints are designed to be the single integration surface for Console (and later, SDK) to retrieve customer-v...

title: Add CI check: release note preview artifact on model metadata diff
preview: Context: As part of the model metadata schema v1 rollout + automated changelog generator effort, we need a consistent reviewer workflow for customer-visible release notes. Today reviewers can merge metadata changes without seeing what will actually be published (Console/docs), and we’ve had at least one incident whe...
text:
Context: As part of the model metadata schema v1 rollout + automated changelog generator effort, we need a consistent reviewer workflow for customer-visible release notes. Today reviewers can merge metadata changes without seeing what will actually be published (Console/docs), and we’ve had at least one incident where internal-only notes briefly leaked due to missing checks.

This PR adds a required CI check that (1) computes a model metadata diff for PRs that touch model metadata/schema/template inputs, (2) generates a release note preview (public + internal) using the same templating/category mapping used by the changelog generator, (3) uploads the preview as a GitHub Actions artifact, and (4) fails the build when required fields or redaction rules are violated.

What this does:
- New workflow: "Release note preview (model metadata)" runs on pull_request for changes matching:
 - mod...

title: openai-v1-chat: param-smoother and tool-output reconciliation to stabilize streaming and logprob alignment
preview: Motivation: customers using the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility layer reported two classes of failures during heavy streaming workloads: 1) fragmented chunking under transient backpressure caused stop tokens and logprob patches to be emitted inconsistently across fragments, producing malformed structured o...
text:
Motivation: customers using the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility layer reported two classes of failures during heavy streaming workloads: 1) fragmented chunking under transient backpressure caused stop tokens and logprob patches to be emitted inconsistently across fragments, producing malformed structured outputs; 2) partial tool/function-call outputs (large JSON blobs emitted over multiple deltas) could be interleaved with text deltas and result in invalid JSON or lost function-call metadata. This PR implements a lightweight "param smoother" and an output reconciliation layer that reduces visible fragmentation to clients while preserving low latency for short responses.

What I changed (summary): consolidated three coordinated changes into one PR to keep the compatibility surface cohesive: 1) stream fragment coalescer: a small in-memory horizon buffer that merges micro-delta...

title: Add structured logging fields + redaction safeguards for Private troubleshooting
preview:
Title: Add structured logging fields + redaction safeguards for Private troubleshooting

Context
Private customers and Support/SRE need a reliable way to correlate failures across the gateway, control plane, and serving runtime without requiring payload logging or environment-specific bespoke parsing. Today, several...
text:
Title: Add structured logging fields + redaction safeguards for Private troubleshooting

Context
Private customers and Support/SRE need a reliable way to correlate failures across the gateway, control plane, and serving runtime without requiring payload logging or environment-specific bespoke parsing. Today, several components log different key names (or omit them entirely), and a few code paths still allow unredacted headers to slip into debug logs.

This PR aligns the log schema for Private installs with the minimum pack expectations and adds redaction safeguards suitable for regulated environments.

Summary of changes
1) Standardized structured fields (JSON logs)
- request_id: propagated from API gateway to downstream control-plane calls and runtime request handling. If an inbound X-Request-Id is present, we honor it (bounded length + charset validation); otherwise generate a UUIDv...

title: Emit audit events for Dedicated autoscaling policy changes + scaling decisions
preview: Summary: This PR adds audit logging for (1) scaling policy configuration changes (min/max/cooldowns/predictive toggles) and (2) autoscaler scaling decisions (requested desired vs bounded desired, reasons, signal inputs). Events are emitted from control plane services and the dedicated autoscaler loop, normalized int...
text:
Summary: This PR adds audit logging for (1) scaling policy configuration changes (min/max/cooldowns/predictive toggles) and (2) autoscaler scaling decisions (requested desired vs bounded desired, reasons, signal inputs). Events are emitted from control plane services and the dedicated autoscaler loop, normalized into a common event shape, and shipped through the existing audit log pipeline for enterprise retention/export.

Why: Dedicated enterprise customers (and internal compliance reviews) need deterministic, reviewable history of who changed bounds and why capacity changed. For the autoscaling bounds + predictive mode rollout we also need post-incident forensics: the ability to answer “what did the autoscaler want”, “what did bounds clamp”, and “which signal(s) caused it”.

Scope:
- New audit event types:
 - dedicated.scaling_policy.updated
 - dedicated.scaling_policy.validation_fa...

title: Console API: add scaling policy endpoints (read/update) w/ RBAC + validation
preview: Summary: Exposes Dedicated deployment scaling policy to the Console via new control-plane endpoints for reading/updating per-deployment scaling policy, including RBAC enforcement and input validation. This unblocks the Console settings page and the internal admin tooling to toggle bounded scaling + predictive mode b...
text: Summary: Exposes Dedicated deployment scaling policy to the Console via new control-plane endpoints for reading/updating per-deployment scaling policy, including RBAC enforcement and input validation. This unblocks the Console settings page and the internal admin tooling to toggle bounded scaling + predictive mode behind flags. Context: Dedicated autoscaling bounds + predictive mode initiative requires customer-configurable min/max and related settings; schema + migrations are in earlier PRs, this PR focuses on the HTTP surface area + authn/authz + validation semantics. Endpoints: (1) GET /v1/console/dedicated/deployments/{deployment_id}/scaling-policy -> returns effective policy + source (explicit vs default) + feature availability; (2) PUT /v1/console/dedicated/deployments/{deployment_id}/scaling-policy -> updates explicit policy for that deployment; (3) POST /v1/console/dedicated/d...

title: CI: add tflint + checkov gates; pin Terraform/AWS provider versions for reproducible builds
preview:
Summary
This PR hardens the `redwood-terraform` alpha module repo by adding Terraform linting + security scanning in CI and by pinning Terraform/AWS provider versions to improve reproducibility across dev machines and CI runners.

Motivation
We’ve had a few sources of drift already during dogfood: different AWS prov...
text:
Summary
This PR hardens the `redwood-terraform` alpha module repo by adding Terraform linting + security scanning in CI and by pinning Terraform/AWS provider versions to improve reproducibility across dev machines and CI runners.

Motivation
We’ve had a few sources of drift already during dogfood: different AWS provider patch versions producing slightly different plans, and “looks fine locally” modules that fail basic best-practice checks once they hit customer environments. Since these modules are explicitly meant to encode Redwood-validated defaults, we should catch issues early and keep outputs stable.

What’s included
- Provider + Terraform version pinning:
 - Add explicit `required_version` and `required_providers` constraints across modules/examples.
 - Standardize on AWS provider `~> 5.0` with a minimum patch level validated in sandbox.
 - Add/refresh `.terraform.lock.hcl` for ...

title: Verify air-gapped upgrade bundles via SHA256 manifest + signature; improve missing/incorrect artifact errors
preview: Context: Design partners and Support have hit confusing failures in the offline/air-gapped upgrade flow when bundles are incomplete, corrupted, or built against the wrong target version (see SUP-18510). Today the installer will often fail late (during image/chart pull) and the error doesn’t clearly tell the operator...
text: Context: Design partners and Support have hit confusing failures in the offline/air-gapped upgrade flow when bundles are incomplete, corrupted, or built against the wrong target version (see SUP-18510). Today the installer will often fail late (during image/chart pull) and the error doesn’t clearly tell the operator what’s missing or mismatched. This PR adds deterministic bundle verification up front: (1) a required SHA256 checksum manifest for all bundle artifacts, (2) optional signature verification for the manifest, and (3) clearer, actionable error messages that enumerate missing/incorrect artifacts before any cluster mutations. Behavior: - New required file inside bundle: bundle.manifest.json (list of artifacts + expected sha256 + size + logical type). - New required file inside bundle: bundle.manifest.sha256 (sha256sum of bundle.manifest.json) OR embedded sha256 in the manifest ...

title: Emit audit log events for Private upgrade lifecycle (start/stop, prechecks, backup, rollback)
preview: This PR adds first-class audit log coverage for Redwood Private upgrade/rollback lifecycle events so enterprise customers can prove change control and correlate operational actions in SIEM. Motivation: Support and SRE have recurring requests for an auditable trail of upgrade attempts (who initiated, what version, wh...
text: This PR adds first-class audit log coverage for Redwood Private upgrade/rollback lifecycle events so enterprise customers can prove change control and correlate operational actions in SIEM. Motivation: Support and SRE have recurring requests for an auditable trail of upgrade attempts (who initiated, what version, which environment, whether prechecks passed, whether backups succeeded, and whether rollback was invoked), especially for regulated Private (VPC/on-prem) deployments. Scope: (1) Adds new audit event types for upgrade lifecycle: private.upgrade.started, private.upgrade.prechecks.completed, private.upgrade.backup.completed, private.upgrade.completed, private.upgrade.failed, private.rollback.invoked, private.rollback.completed, private.rollback.failed. (2) Wires emission into the Private upgrade coordinator/state machine so events are emitted exactly-once per step transition (id...

title: Create HubSpot client wrapper with retry, rate-limit handling, and token refresh
preview:
## Summary
This PR introduces a shared HubSpot API client wrapper used by the PLG signal router service (ENG-7750). The goal is to make HubSpot writes reliable under bursty load and HubSpot API rate limits, while keeping behavior deterministic and auditable.

Key features:
- OAuth access token refresh (using refresh...
text:
## Summary
This PR introduces a shared HubSpot API client wrapper used by the PLG signal router service (ENG-7750). The goal is to make HubSpot writes reliable under bursty load and HubSpot API rate limits, while keeping behavior deterministic and auditable.

Key features:
- OAuth access token refresh (using refresh token) with concurrency-safe caching
- Exponential backoff + jitter for transient errors (429/5xx/network)
- First-class handling for HubSpot rate-limit headers (and Retry-After when present)
- Structured logs + request correlation for audit/debug
- Small adapter layer for common Company operations used by the router (get-by-domain, upsert properties, create engagement note, create task)

## Context / Motivation
During early wiring of the signal router → HubSpot integration, we observed:
- HubSpot returns 429s under modest concurrency even when we are under our own QPS exp...

title: Burst: rollout toggle by region + hardware class (allow-list) with per-tenant enablement + audit trail
preview: Context: We need a safe GA ramp for Dedicated burst capacity, staged by region + hardware class (ex: us-east/H100 first) and further gated by explicit per-tenant enablement. This PR adds a control-plane enforced rollout allow-list keyed by (region, hardware_class) and ties it into the existing tenant burst config. I...
text:
Context: We need a safe GA ramp for Dedicated burst capacity, staged by region + hardware class (ex: us-east/H100 first) and further gated by explicit per-tenant enablement. This PR adds a control-plane enforced rollout allow-list keyed by (region, hardware_class) and ties it into the existing tenant burst config. It also emits audit events for all rollout policy changes, with RBAC restrictions so only privileged operators can modify the allow-list.

Key behavior:
- A tenant can be configured for burst (tenant.burst.enabled=true), but burst will not be admitted unless the fleet rollout allow-list includes the tenant’s region + hardware_class.
- Allow-list is evaluated deterministically in the control plane policy layer and returned as part of the admission precheck metadata so routing can surface consistent denial reason codes.
- Rollout policy changes are auditable (who/when/what cha...

title: GA: enable route experiments by default + tighten validations + release notes
preview:
Summary
This PR flips the GA defaults for route-level experiments (Console Rollouts) and tightens validations across the control plane + policy evaluator, plus wires up release notes + rollout checklist for the 2025.04 train.

Context
During beta we saw two recurring issues:
1) Variant skew when a stable stickiness ...
text:
Summary
This PR flips the GA defaults for route-level experiments (Console Rollouts) and tightens validations across the control plane + policy evaluator, plus wires up release notes + rollout checklist for the 2025.04 train.

Context
During beta we saw two recurring issues:
1) Variant skew when a stable stickiness key was missing or low-entropy (SUP-24831, see also follow-up fixes in pr-28705).
2) Customers accidentally creating configs that were "valid" structurally but unsafe operationally (overlapping experiments, impossible allocations, incompatible model variants, and confusing stop semantics).

This PR is intentionally not adding statistical significance analysis (explicit non-goal for GA per PM-482). It focuses on making the defaults safe and predictable.

What changed (high level)
- GA enablement defaults:
 - Route experiments are enabled by default for eligible orgs behind a...

title: RBAC: add permissions and default roles for SIEM audit export management
preview: Implements least-privilege RBAC permissions/scopes for the SIEM-ready audit export beta. This PR introduces explicit permissions for (a) managing audit export configurations (create/update/delete/enable/disable), (b) rotating/attaching destination credentials, and (c) viewing exporter delivery health/status (lag, la...
text:
Implements least-privilege RBAC permissions/scopes for the SIEM-ready audit export beta. This PR introduces explicit permissions for (a) managing audit export configurations (create/update/delete/enable/disable), (b) rotating/attaching destination credentials, and (c) viewing exporter delivery health/status (lag, last success, last error) in Console and via API. Permissions are wired through the control-plane authz layer and included in default enterprise roles with feature-flag gating. Linked: ENG-5173.

Motivation: We need to allow Security/Compliance and Workspace Admins to manage export destinations without granting broad platform-admin access, while ensuring auditors/support can view delivery health without being able to exfiltrate secrets or modify destinations.

Scope:
- New permission names + descriptions in the RBAC registry
- Policy checks in the control-plane endpoints for ...

title: Admin override for onboarding checklist (Support/CS) + audit logging
preview: Context: Support/CS needs a safe way to mark checklist steps complete/reset during onboarding troubleshooting (e.g., customer rotated keys, telemetry backfill lag, or a step was missed due to earlier event-ordering issues). This PR adds admin-only override endpoints to the checklist progress service, plus audit logg...
text:
Context: Support/CS needs a safe way to mark checklist steps complete/reset during onboarding troubleshooting (e.g., customer rotated keys, telemetry backfill lag, or a step was missed due to earlier event-ordering issues). This PR adds admin-only override endpoints to the checklist progress service, plus audit logging and minimal UI plumbing for internal tools (Console will consume in a follow-up PR).

What’s included: (1) Admin override API endpoints to mark a checklist step COMPLETE/NOT_STARTED (or clear an override) for a tenant, with optional annotation text and an internal reason code; (2) Strict authorization (RBAC) so only Support/CS roles can call the endpoints, scoped to internal operators; (3) Immutable audit log entries capturing actor, tenant, step, previous state, new state, reason, and timestamp; (4) Telemetry events for override actions (PII-free) so we can monitor fre...

title: Control plane: audit export config CRUD endpoints + validation (feature-flagged)
preview: Implements the first-cut control-plane API surface for SIEM-ready audit export config management, gated behind the `siem_audit_export_beta` feature flag. This PR adds CRUD endpoints for export configurations (S3 / Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs), request validation (destination-specific, filter schema, and backfill windo...
text: Implements the first-cut control-plane API surface for SIEM-ready audit export config management, gated behind the `siem_audit_export_beta` feature flag. This PR adds CRUD endpoints for export configurations (S3 / Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs), request validation (destination-specific, filter schema, and backfill window constraints), and an audit trail for config changes (create/update/disable/delete + credential rotation reference updates). The API is intended to be consumed by Console (settings page) and by the exporter service config sync. Notes: filtering evaluation stays in the exporter for now, but we validate filter shape + safe defaults here to prevent pathological configs. RBAC integration is stubbed to existing admin checks in this PR; fine-grained permissions land in follow-up (ENG-5173 / PR-28792). Endpoints: `GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/audit-exports/configs` (list), `...

title: RBAC v2: add end-to-end integration tests for critical permissions (keys, roles, routing policies)
preview: Context: As part of RBAC v2 rollout we’ve been relying on unit tests + endpoint annotation coverage, but we’ve still had a few regressions where critical routes either (a) were reachable through alternate gateway paths (streaming vs non-streaming), or (b) had inconsistent enforcement between gateway and control-plan...
text: Context: As part of RBAC v2 rollout we’ve been relying on unit tests + endpoint annotation coverage, but we’ve still had a few regressions where critical routes either (a) were reachable through alternate gateway paths (streaming vs non-streaming), or (b) had inconsistent enforcement between gateway and control-plane services. This PR adds end-to-end integration tests that exercise the full request path (gateway -> service) and validates allow/deny decisions for a small set of high-risk actions: API key rotation, role updates/bindings, and routing policy edits. What’s included: (1) New test harness utilities to create org/project/env fixtures and issue signed test tokens for users with specific RBAC v2 role bindings. (2) E2E tests for API key rotation endpoints: verifies that users without `keys.rotate` (or equivalent) receive 403 with structured denial code; verifies that a user with...

title: Add audit export permissions + default role mappings (enterprise admin vs auditor)
preview:
Summary
This updates the rbac-policy-bundle to include new permissions required for SIEM-ready audit export configuration + operational visibility. It also updates default role mappings to keep least-privilege separation between enterprise admins (manage exports) and auditors (read-only + status).

Context
The contr...
text:
Summary
This updates the rbac-policy-bundle to include new permissions required for SIEM-ready audit export configuration + operational visibility. It also updates default role mappings to keep least-privilege separation between enterprise admins (manage exports) and auditors (read-only + status).

Context
The control plane and Console are introducing audit export CRUD + delivery status endpoints behind a beta feature flag. This PR updates the central policy bundle so permissions are standardized across Hosted and Dedicated. Private/on-prem roles are intentionally unchanged for beta.

Linked work
- ENG-5173 (RBAC permissions for export management)
- Control plane endpoints: redwood/pr-28792 (RBAC) + redwood/pr-28766 (config API) (FYI)

What changed
1) New permission set (names + semantics)
- audit_exports.read
 - Allows viewing export configurations (destination type, enabled/disabled...

title: Audit export: add experiment identifiers, actor details, and change diff summary
preview: Context: Enterprise customers (and internal Security) need exported audit events to be SIEM-ready for Route Experiments. Today our export stream includes event_type + timestamps but is missing experiment identifiers and a human-readable change summary, which makes it hard to answer “who changed what experiment confi...
text:
Context: Enterprise customers (and internal Security) need exported audit events to be SIEM-ready for Route Experiments. Today our export stream includes event_type + timestamps but is missing experiment identifiers and a human-readable change summary, which makes it hard to answer “who changed what experiment config, when, and from/to what?”.

This PR extends the audit export format (NDJSON) with:
- experiment_id, route_id, and (where applicable) variant_id
- actor fields (actor_id, actor_email, actor_type, impersonated_by) aligned with existing authn payloads
- change_diff_summary: a compact, redacted summary of the config diff for UPDATE events (and lifecycle transitions for START/STOP)

Notes:
- This is export-only; the underlying audit event payload stored in the audit log remains the source of truth.
- PII handling: change_diff_summary is explicitly redacted for known sensitive ...

title: Include authz decision context in SIEM export (ECS/CIM mapping)
preview:
Summary
Adds support for exporting RBAC v2 authorization decision context fields (subject/action/resource/scope/policy_version/decision/denial reasons) through the audit-log-exporter and maps them to common SIEM schema conventions.

This PR is intended to unblock enterprise security questionnaire asks and pilot cust...
text:
Summary
Adds support for exporting RBAC v2 authorization decision context fields (subject/action/resource/scope/policy_version/decision/denial reasons) through the audit-log-exporter and maps them to common SIEM schema conventions.

This PR is intended to unblock enterprise security questionnaire asks and pilot customer requirements where SIEM rules need to distinguish auth failures (deny) from application errors, and correlate access decisions with specific permission strings.

Context
- Redwood platform services are adding authz decision fields to audit log events (see redwood monorepo PR: redwood#28292).
- RBAC v2 requires evidence-friendly audit trails (SOC2 + enterprise questionnaires).
- Customers commonly ingest our audit logs into Splunk (CIM) and/or Elastic (ECS). They want stable field names, consistent typing, and predictable nullability.

What changed
1) New exported field...

title: Add Private deployment operator + auditor role templates (env/cluster scoped)
preview: Summary: This PR adds a set of built-in RBAC v2 role templates intended for Redwood Private deployments, focused on common operator and auditor workflows. The templates are explicitly scoped (environment and cluster) and are designed to support least-privilege, separation of duties, and evidence-friendly auditabilit...
text: Summary: This PR adds a set of built-in RBAC v2 role templates intended for Redwood Private deployments, focused on common operator and auditor workflows. The templates are explicitly scoped (environment and cluster) and are designed to support least-privilege, separation of duties, and evidence-friendly auditability. Context/motivation: Private customers (and internal pilots) have asked for clear “day-2 operations” roles (upgrade, health checks, log viewing) and separate read-only/audit roles that can export audit logs without granting mutation privileges. We also saw multiple support cases where Dedicated/Private admins were blocked due to missing template permissions or where customers over-granted Org Admin to solve an operator need (SUP-48288, SUP-48219). This PR is part of ENG-18444 (role template generator + templates) and aligns with the scope model decisions being rolled into...

title: Policy engine: guardrails for rollout actions (step size, soak time, approvals)
preview: Context: Hosted canary rollouts GA requires enforceable guardrails on *actions* (create/start/progress/pause/resume/rollback) so customers can’t accidentally (or maliciously) exceed approved step sizes, skip minimum soak times, or bypass required approvals. This PR adds rollout-specific policy checks to the policy e...
text: Context: Hosted canary rollouts GA requires enforceable guardrails on *actions* (create/start/progress/pause/resume/rollback) so customers can’t accidentally (or maliciously) exceed approved step sizes, skip minimum soak times, or bypass required approvals. This PR adds rollout-specific policy checks to the policy engine and wires them into the Console backend error contract (human-readable messages + stable error codes). Scope: (1) step size guardrails: max traffic jump per step (e.g., 1%→25% disallowed if max_step_increase_pct=10) with special-case handling for rollback; (2) soak time guardrails: minimum soak duration between step changes, with explicit override only when the actor has a higher-privilege permission; (3) approval gates: per-action approvals (e.g., requiring rollout_approver for start and/or step changes past N%) with consistent denial semantics; (4) error mapping: po...

title: Rollouts: persist activity timeline + emit audit log events for rollout actions
preview:
Context
We need a GA-grade, customer-visible timeline of what happened during a Hosted canary rollout, and we need security-grade audit log coverage for the same set of actions. Today we have some internal orchestrator events, but they are (a) not normalized for Console display and (b) not emitted to the audit pipel...
text:
Context
We need a GA-grade, customer-visible timeline of what happened during a Hosted canary rollout, and we need security-grade audit log coverage for the same set of actions. Today we have some internal orchestrator events, but they are (a) not normalized for Console display and (b) not emitted to the audit pipeline consistently.

This PR adds:
1) A persisted rollout activity timeline (customer visible) stored alongside rollout state.
2) Audit log emission for rollout actions with consistent payloads + diffing.

Scope
- Rollout actions covered: create, start, pause, resume, step_change (traffic percentage), approve (where approval gate is enabled), rollback, auto_pause/auto_rollback (stop-condition driven), and config edits that affect the rollout plan.
- Activity timeline is stored as an append-only log with a small canonical schema and rendered by Console (DES-755).
- Audit log e...

title: Security: destination validation + egress allowlist hooks (SSRF/exfil mitigation)
preview: Motivation: This PR adds a defense-in-depth validation layer in the exporter to reduce SSRF and data-exfiltration risk by (a) validating destination endpoints for Splunk HEC and Datadog prior to use and (b) adding an egress allowlist hook that can be enforced by the runtime environment. We already validate some dest...
text: Motivation: This PR adds a defense-in-depth validation layer in the exporter to reduce SSRF and data-exfiltration risk by (a) validating destination endpoints for Splunk HEC and Datadog prior to use and (b) adding an egress allowlist hook that can be enforced by the runtime environment. We already validate some destination fields in the control plane, but we want the exporter to be safe even if a bad config slips through (migration bugs, stale cached config, or future destinations). Summary of changes: 1) Strict URL parsing + normalization: Introduce a shared DestinationURL parser that rejects ambiguous/invalid URLs, strips userinfo, normalizes scheme/host/port, and prevents path tricks (e.g., embedded @, backslashes, encoded host separators). 2) Blocklist for dangerous targets: Reject localhost, link-local, and RFC1918 / private ranges (including 127.0.0.0/8, ::1, 10.0.0.0/8, 172.16....

title: Audit logging: emit experiment lifecycle events (CRUD/start/stop) with redaction + diff summary
preview: Context: Route-level experiments are now mutable via Console APIs (create/update/start/stop/delete). Enterprise customers and our own compliance posture require an immutable audit trail for configuration changes and lifecycle actions with a predictable schema and safe redaction behavior. This PR adds first-class aud...
text:
Context: Route-level experiments are now mutable via Console APIs (create/update/start/stop/delete). Enterprise customers and our own compliance posture require an immutable audit trail for configuration changes and lifecycle actions with a predictable schema and safe redaction behavior. This PR adds first-class audit events for experiment lifecycle, wired into the existing AuditLog pipeline, and aligns fields with Security’s requirements doc. Ties to ENG-9030.

What’s in this PR: Adds audit events for the experiment lifecycle actions in the experiment control plane + Console backend APIs: experiment.create, experiment.update, experiment.start, experiment.stop, experiment.delete. Each event includes the required identifiers and a compact change summary while avoiding logging sensitive config fields verbatim. Implements deterministic redaction rules for the experiment spec (especially ...

title: Add internal debug endpoint to explain route experiment assignment decisions
preview:
## Summary
Adds an internal debug endpoint that explains how the policy engine / router evaluated route-level experiments for a request and why a particular variant was selected. This is intended for on-call + Support escalations where we suspect skewed allocation, missing/low-entropy stickiness keys, or config prop...
text:
## Summary
Adds an internal debug endpoint that explains how the policy engine / router evaluated route-level experiments for a request and why a particular variant was selected. This is intended for on-call + Support escalations where we suspect skewed allocation, missing/low-entropy stickiness keys, or config propagation issues.

Endpoint returns: experiment_id, variant_id, selected variant reason, config version + age, the stickiness key resolution path, hash inputs (redacted), computed bucket value, and any precedence/fallback interactions.

## Motivation
During beta we’ve had multiple cases where customers report “variant skew” (e.g., 95/5 split behaving like 100/0) or Console results appear stale. Today we can infer a lot from logs/metrics, but we lack a single place to answer: “What did the router decide for this request, using which config, and based on which identifiers?”

Th...

title: Add feature flag gates for route experiments (beta enablement + rollback)
preview: Summary: Introduces first-class feature flag gates for Route-level A/B testing (“route experiments”) across the control plane and data plane so we can: (1) enable beta accounts safely (allowlist), (2) do region-by-region rollout, and (3) have a single kill switch for emergency rollback. This PR does not change exper...
text: Summary: Introduces first-class feature flag gates for Route-level A/B testing (“route experiments”) across the control plane and data plane so we can: (1) enable beta accounts safely (allowlist), (2) do region-by-region rollout, and (3) have a single kill switch for emergency rollback. This PR does not change experiment semantics; it only gates evaluation + API surfacing. Context/why: As we expand beta (Phase 2) we need a deterministic, auditable enablement mechanism that does not require config churn or code deploys. We also want to avoid partial enablement (Console shows experiments but router ignores them, or vice versa). This PR aligns gating across: policy engine evaluator, router request context, and Console APIs. Rollout plan references: ENG-9036 release train plan + internal thread “feature-flag plan for beta enablement” (eng-releases/1736700299). What’s included: (1) New fla...

title: Add audit log events for Dedicated burst config changes
preview:
Context
Dedicated burst capacity introduces tenant-scoped configuration (enablement, caps, tiers, and temporary overrides). For enterprise + regulated customers, Security/Compliance requires auditable evidence of who changed what, when, and from where (Console vs API vs internal tooling), including support/admin ove...
text:
Context
Dedicated burst capacity introduces tenant-scoped configuration (enablement, caps, tiers, and temporary overrides). For enterprise + regulated customers, Security/Compliance requires auditable evidence of who changed what, when, and from where (Console vs API vs internal tooling), including support/admin overrides.

This PR adds first-class audit log events for burst configuration mutations and ties them to RBAC permissions, so both external customer admins and Redwood operators generate consistent audit records.

What’s included
- New audit events emitted on tenant burst config writes:
 - dedicated.burst.enable_updated (enable/disable)
 - dedicated.burst.limits_updated (max burst ratio / absolute cap / cooldown window)
 - dedicated.burst.priority_tier_updated (gold/silver/standard)
 - dedicated.burst.override_applied (temporary override with TTL)
 - dedicated.burst.override_r...

title: RBAC: gate route experiment mutations in Console APIs
preview:
## Summary
This PR enforces RBAC checks on all route experiment *mutation* endpoints exposed by the Console backend (create/update/start/stop/delete, plus traffic split updates). Read-only endpoints (list/get/results) remain unchanged.

Motivation: route-level experiments change request routing behavior and can impa...
text:
## Summary
This PR enforces RBAC checks on all route experiment *mutation* endpoints exposed by the Console backend (create/update/start/stop/delete, plus traffic split updates). Read-only endpoints (list/get/results) remain unchanged.

Motivation: route-level experiments change request routing behavior and can impact latency/cost/reliability. For enterprise accounts we need consistent authz semantics with Rollouts and audit logging requirements.

## What changed
- Added explicit permission gates to experiment mutation handlers using the existing Console authz middleware.
- Introduced a small permission mapping for experiment operations (fine-grained but aligned with existing admin roles).
- Added integration tests covering:
 - 403 on mutation attempts without required permissions
 - 200/201 with the correct permissions
 - error payload includes a stable `error_code` to drive UX copy ...

title: Add checklist progress API (state read + internal event ingest) for onboarding checklist MVP
preview: Context: We’re adding an in-console onboarding checklist (“first production launch”) and need a stable backend contract for (a) fetching the current checklist state for a workspace/tenant and (b) ingesting server-side onboarding events emitted by gateway/control plane. This PR introduces a small checklist progress A...
text: Context: We’re adding an in-console onboarding checklist (“first production launch”) and need a stable backend contract for (a) fetching the current checklist state for a workspace/tenant and (b) ingesting server-side onboarding events emitted by gateway/control plane. This PR introduces a small checklist progress API service surface (behind internal auth) plus basic unit tests. Scope: - Read API for Console: GET checklist state + computed completion (per-step state, timestamps, completion percent, and next recommended step). - Write-only internal ingestion API: POST onboarding events (server-to-server only) with idempotency keys; persists raw events and updates step state. - Computation: event-driven completion with monotonic transitions (never regress a step from COMPLETE->INCOMPLETE) and deterministic ordering by event_time + ingest_time fallback. - Security/privacy: payload is res...

title: Audit logs: include authz decision context + policy version identifiers
preview:
## Summary
This PR extends audit log events emitted by Redwood services (API Gateway + Control Plane) to include authorization decision context when available. The goal is to make authorization enforcement auditable (SOC2 evidence, enterprise questionnaires) and to support debugging/forensics ("who attempted what", ...
text:
## Summary
This PR extends audit log events emitted by Redwood services (API Gateway + Control Plane) to include authorization decision context when available. The goal is to make authorization enforcement auditable (SOC2 evidence, enterprise questionnaires) and to support debugging/forensics ("who attempted what", "why denied", "which policy version was evaluated").

This is intentionally additive: existing event consumers continue to work without changes. Fields are only populated when we have a request-scoped authz evaluation result.

## Motivation / context
RBAC v2 rollout requires: (1) deny-by-default enforcement, and (2) evidence-friendly audit artifacts that tie an audited action to an explicit authz decision and policy version. Several enterprise prospects asked for examples of audit entries containing allow/deny, subject identity, scoped resource, and policy version identifie...

title: Add rollout-approver role + permissions for Hosted canary rollouts
preview: Summary: Introduces a dedicated `rollout-approver` role and updates permission mappings to support the Hosted canary rollout workflow GA. This role is intended for orgs that want separation of duties: engineers can draft/create rollouts, but a smaller set of users must approve step changes (or rollback) when approva...
text:
Summary: Introduces a dedicated `rollout-approver` role and updates permission mappings to support the Hosted canary rollout workflow GA. This role is intended for orgs that want separation of duties: engineers can draft/create rollouts, but a smaller set of users must approve step changes (or rollback) when approval gates are enabled by policy.

Context / why: The canary workflow adds new actions that need explicit RBAC coverage: create rollout, start rollout, progress step (increase canary %), approve gated step, pause, rollback/cancel. Product + Security requested an explicit approver role rather than overloading existing admin roles, and Support requested clearer error messages when customers lack permissions.

What’s in this PR: Adds new role definition `rollout_approver` (exposed as `rollout-approver` in Console) and wires the following permissions: - `rollouts.read`: view rollo...

title: Add global kill-switch config for quantization profiles (routing + runtime propagation)
preview: Summary: Introduces a platform-level kill-switch to disable Quantization Profile application globally, regardless of per-route settings. This is intended for emergency response (quality regression, incompatibility bug, or unexpected runtime instability) and is designed to be deterministic, auditable, and fast to pro...
text:
Summary: Introduces a platform-level kill-switch to disable Quantization Profile application globally, regardless of per-route settings. This is intended for emergency response (quality regression, incompatibility bug, or unexpected runtime instability) and is designed to be deterministic, auditable, and fast to propagate.

Context/Motivation: Route-level enablement is the default control for v1, but we need a single knob for SRE/oncall to quickly halt profile application without editing individual route configs. This PR adds a config flag that is evaluated in the control-plane resolution path and propagated through routing to the runtime. When the kill-switch is active, the resolved quantization outcome is forced to baseline and we record a consistent reason code.

Design goals: (1) Default behavior unchanged when kill-switch is off. (2) Deterministic: the same request under the same...
links: ADR-0137 (route-level enablement) referenced for non-goal: not changing route semantics
Internal runbook: quant-profiles-rollback.md (updated in this PR)

title: Support attaching retention config export to audit log evidence package
preview:
Context
Customers (and GRC internally) frequently request both (a) audit logs for a specified window and (b) an export of the tenant’s retention configuration (incl. defaults + overrides + change history pointer) as a single evidence package. This PR adds a first-class way to bundle the retention config export along...
text:
Context
Customers (and GRC internally) frequently request both (a) audit logs for a specified window and (b) an export of the tenant’s retention configuration (incl. defaults + overrides + change history pointer) as a single evidence package. This PR adds a first-class way to bundle the retention config export alongside the audit log export output.

Scope
- Add optional attachment support to evidence packages produced by audit-log-exporter.
- Implement a RetentionConfigAttachmentProvider which fetches retention config export from the control plane export endpoint and embeds it in the package.
- Update report template runner to allow "attachments" definitions (initially: retention_config).
- Add guardrails: size limits, explicit allowlist, and redaction check for known sensitive fields.
- Update CLI + HTTP API to accept the attachment flag(s).

Design notes
- Attachments are stored wit...

title: gateway: return 409 + standardized error payload for residency policy violations
preview:
Context
We’re introducing Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning) across routing + gateway. The gateway must return a deterministic, consistent error when a request is blocked by residency policy (instead of falling back, timing out, or returning generic 500s in some streaming paths).

This PR implements ...
text:
Context
We’re introducing Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning) across routing + gateway. The gateway must return a deterministic, consistent error when a request is blocked by residency policy (instead of falling back, timing out, or returning generic 500s in some streaming paths).

This PR implements the public error contract for residency violations:
- Returns HTTP 409 (Conflict) when residency policy blocks a request
- Standardized JSON error payload with stable `error.code`, `error.type`, and `help_url`
- Works consistently for both streaming and non-streaming endpoints
- Propagates `request_id` and `residency.decision_id` for support/debug (without exposing full policy content)
- Maps internal policy-engine outcomes to a small set of public-facing reason codes

Motivation / user impact
- Customers with EU-only pinning should get a clear, actionable error when their ...

title: TTL enforcement for audit logs store (retention policy wiring + purge job)
preview: Implements retention/TTL enforcement for the audit logs storage layer and wires it into the control-plane retention policy evaluator. This is a core dependency for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” project (ENG-18434). Summary: (1) Adds a background purge job for audit logs using the purge job fra...
text:
Implements retention/TTL enforcement for the audit logs storage layer and wires it into the control-plane retention policy evaluator. This is a core dependency for the “Retention controls + compliance reporting pack” project (ENG-18434). Summary: (1) Adds a background purge job for audit logs using the purge job framework (PR #28489). (2) Evaluates effective retention policy per tenant + region + data class (audit_logs) via the control plane evaluator (ADR-017 / PR #28427). (3) Enforces safe defaults and guardrails: minimum TTL by tier, optional legal hold override (no deletes while hold is active), and deletion safety limits to avoid DB pressure. (4) Adds verification metrics for TTL lag + deletion counts + failures with labels constrained to avoid cardinality blowups. (5) Adds integration tests covering policy changes, cache invalidation, and idempotent purge runs.

Implementation d...

title: Wire role/scopes into config diff access + Sev0/Sev1 break-glass flow
preview: Summary: This PR completes the permissioning work for the tenant-aware config diff endpoint by wiring AuthN/AuthZ roles + OAuth scopes into the handler and adding a time-bounded break-glass path for on-call responders during Sev0/Sev1 incidents. The intent is to make incident-bot v2 and Console diagnostics safe-by-d...
text:
Summary: This PR completes the permissioning work for the tenant-aware config diff endpoint by wiring AuthN/AuthZ roles + OAuth scopes into the handler and adding a time-bounded break-glass path for on-call responders during Sev0/Sev1 incidents. The intent is to make incident-bot v2 and Console diagnostics safe-by-default (least privilege, customer-safe output) while still enabling responders to unblock themselves in emergencies with explicit audit trails.

Context: We introduced the config diff endpoint in PR-29112 with redaction hooks and basic authorization plumbing, but we were still relying on coarse environment allowlists. During the 2025-02-07 Hosted API incident followups and INT-3312 we identified gaps: (a) bot lacked a narrowly-scoped permission to call diff in prod, (b) SREs needed a documented break-glass procedure for Sev0/Sev1, and (c) Security required auditable, severi...

title: Control plane: API endpoints for route-level quantization settings (CRUD + RBAC + audit log)
preview:
Motivation
We need a control-plane API surface to store and manage route-level quantization settings for the Quantization Profile Library (v1). This PR adds the first pass at CRUD endpoints, enforces RBAC, and emits audit records so changes are attributable and reversible.

Scope
- Adds RouteQuantizationSettings res...
text:
Motivation
We need a control-plane API surface to store and manage route-level quantization settings for the Quantization Profile Library (v1). This PR adds the first pass at CRUD endpoints, enforces RBAC, and emits audit records so changes are attributable and reversible.

Scope
- Adds RouteQuantizationSettings resource and persistence (backed by existing route_config storage).
- API endpoints:
 - GET /v1/routes/{route_id}/quantization-settings
 - PUT /v1/routes/{route_id}/quantization-settings (upsert)
 - DELETE /v1/routes/{route_id}/quantization-settings (reset to default)
 - GET /v1/orgs/{org_id}/routes/quantization-settings (bulk list for Console)
- RBAC: restricted to org_owner, org_admin, and route_admin roles (same authorization model used for route config edits).
- Audit logging: emits an audit record on create/update/delete with actor, org_id, route_id, and diff summary.
- V...

title: Redact secrets in config diffs before rendering to Slack
preview: Problem: Incident-bot v2 is starting to surface tenant-safe config diffs in #incidents and #eng-oncall, but the current formatting is purely syntactic and can leak secrets if upstream config contains inline credentials/tokens (or if a value looks like a secret). This PR adds a deterministic redaction layer that runs...
text: Problem: Incident-bot v2 is starting to surface tenant-safe config diffs in #incidents and #eng-oncall, but the current formatting is purely syntactic and can leak secrets if upstream config contains inline credentials/tokens (or if a value looks like a secret). This PR adds a deterministic redaction layer that runs before any diff content is rendered to Slack (cards, modals, and copy-to-clipboard). Approach: (1) Add key-based redaction (denylist of sensitive key names + path-based matches). (2) Add value-based secret detection (JWTs, API keys, common token prefixes, PEM blocks, base64-ish long strings, AWS-like access keys, etc.) with conservative thresholds. (3) Add allowlisted keys/paths that are safe to show even if they trip generic heuristics (e.g., numeric limits, routing weights, non-sensitive feature flag booleans). (4) Replace redacted values with stable placeholders ("[REDA...

title: Improve rate limits + circuit breakers for diagnostics endpoints (incident-bot/Console)
preview: Context: During incidents we see a spike in diagnostics traffic driven by incident-bot v2 thread interactions + Console deep links. When upstream telemetry/config systems are degraded, retries/timeouts can turn into a burst that (a) increases tail latencies for responders and (b) adds load to already-sick dependenci...
text: Context: During incidents we see a spike in diagnostics traffic driven by incident-bot v2 thread interactions + Console deep links. When upstream telemetry/config systems are degraded, retries/timeouts can turn into a burst that (a) increases tail latencies for responders and (b) adds load to already-sick dependencies. This PR adds explicit rate limits and circuit breakers to the diagnostics surface area (telemetry aggregation, config diff, and deploy metadata) to prevent self-inflicted load while still keeping the bot/Console useful in degraded conditions. Changes: (1) Introduce a shared "Diagnostics Guard" middleware that applies token-bucket limits (per service identity, per endpoint, and per tenant when available) with a small global safety cap; returns bounded responses with retry-after hints. (2) Add a dependency-aware circuit breaker around outbound calls to telemetry-query, co...

title: Add time-windowed exports + actor/event-type filters (report templates)
preview:
## Summary
This PR enhances `audit-log-exporter` to support compliance/evidence workflows by adding (1) explicit time-windowed exports and (2) optional filters for actor + event types. It also introduces a small report-template layer so the compliance reporting pack can request a known preset ("last_90_days_admin_ac...
text:
## Summary
This PR enhances `audit-log-exporter` to support compliance/evidence workflows by adding (1) explicit time-windowed exports and (2) optional filters for actor + event types. It also introduces a small report-template layer so the compliance reporting pack can request a known preset ("last_90_days_admin_activity", "rbac_changes", etc.) without re-encoding filter logic in multiple places.

Motivation: enterprise security reviews are consistently asking for "audit logs for a defined period" and "only admin/security-relevant events". We also had a support report (SUP-3917) where customers believed exports were missing event types; part of that was unclear defaults and lack of an explicit event-type filter list in the export request.

## API changes
### New request fields (non-breaking)
- `start_time` (RFC3339, inclusive)
- `end_time` (RFC3339, exclusive)
- `actor_ids` (optional...

title: Add internal feature flag + RBAC gating for Unit Economics dashboard
preview: Context: The new internal Unit Economics dashboard surfaces sensitive cost data (blended $/1M output tokens, drilldowns by model/region/tier). We need to ensure access is restricted to a small internal allowlist initially and that all access is auditable for Finance and Security sign-off. This PR adds (1) a server-s...
text: Context: The new internal Unit Economics dashboard surfaces sensitive cost data (blended $/1M output tokens, drilldowns by model/region/tier). We need to ensure access is restricted to a small internal allowlist initially and that all access is auditable for Finance and Security sign-off. This PR adds (1) a server-side feature flag gate and (2) RBAC policy updates + enforcement in the Console backend so that the Unit Economics endpoints and UI route are not reachable unless explicitly granted. Changes: - Feature flag: add `console.internal_unit_economics_dashboard` (server-side, env-scoped). Default OFF in all environments; can be enabled in prod only for the internal allowlist group. - RBAC: introduce permission `console:unit_economics:read` and require it for all unit economics API endpoints + the console route loader. - Enforcement points: apply gating at the HTTP router layer and ...

title: Control Plane: annotate sensitive endpoints with RBAC v2 permissions + coverage tests
preview: Context: As part of RBAC v2, we need a canonical mapping from Control Plane API actions to permission strings and a mechanical way to ensure new endpoints can’t ship without being categorized. This PR adds explicit permission annotations to the most security-relevant Control Plane routes (org/user/role management an...
text: Context: As part of RBAC v2, we need a canonical mapping from Control Plane API actions to permission strings and a mechanical way to ensure new endpoints can’t ship without being categorized. This PR adds explicit permission annotations to the most security-relevant Control Plane routes (org/user/role management and routing policy updates) and introduces unit tests that fail if a sensitive endpoint is missing a permission. This is intended to support deny-by-default enforcement in the gateway/service middleware (separate PRs), and to improve auditability by guaranteeing an action string exists for every sensitive operation. Scope: (1) Add @RequiresPermission(...) annotations for org/user/role CRUD, API key admin surfaces that live in the Control Plane, and routing policy create/update/delete + rollout/pinning operations. (2) Introduce an endpoint coverage test that enumerates registe...

title: Backpressure controls + max lag safety (auto-pause) for downstream outages
preview: Context: We had a beta incident where Splunk HEC throttling (429 + intermittent 503) caused exporter lag to spike and sustained retries increased queue pressure and CPU, impacting other tenants sharing the same exporter shard (see #incidents thread 1739559010 and SUP-18531 follow-up). This PR introduces explicit bac...
text:
Context: We had a beta incident where Splunk HEC throttling (429 + intermittent 503) caused exporter lag to spike and sustained retries increased queue pressure and CPU, impacting other tenants sharing the same exporter shard (see #incidents thread 1739559010 and SUP-18531 follow-up). This PR introduces explicit backpressure controls and a max-lag safety mechanism to keep the system stable during downstream outages while preserving at-least-once semantics.

What’s in this PR: (1) Max lag threshold per destination with auto-pause: if export_lag_seconds for a (tenant, destination) exceeds configurable max_lag_seconds for a sustained window, we stop pulling new batches for that destination and mark it PAUSED (reason=MAX_LAG) while continuing to process other destinations/tenants. (2) Backpressure based on in-flight bytes and retry backlog: introduce per-destination in_flight_bytes limits...

title: Add config diff collector (config-service integration) + Slack formatting
preview:
Summary
This PR wires Incident Bot v2 into the new config-service diff endpoint and adds a first-pass Slack-safe rendering for config diffs. The goal is to surface tenant-safe config deltas (feature flags, routing policy, quotas/rate limits, model pins) in the first 5 minutes of an incident without responders needin...
text:
Summary
This PR wires Incident Bot v2 into the new config-service diff endpoint and adds a first-pass Slack-safe rendering for config diffs. The goal is to surface tenant-safe config deltas (feature flags, routing policy, quotas/rate limits, model pins) in the first 5 minutes of an incident without responders needing to jump through manual queries.

Scope
- Adds a ConfigDiffCollector that calls config-service (internal) to fetch a bounded diff for a (tenant, service, region, time window).
- Adds basic formatting for Slack: compact summary card + optional “expand” view payload (used by later Slack modal work).
- Adds guardrails: query timeouts, size limits, allowlisted keys (with explicit “redacted/omitted” markers), and stable ordering to reduce diff noise.
- Adds audit context headers (requestor + incident thread id) so config-service can emit audit events (event emission handled in ...

title: Generate built-in role templates from YAML + linting + versioned release artifacts
preview: Context: As part of RBAC v2 we want built-in role templates (Org Admin, Security Admin, Read-only Auditor, Deployment Operator, etc.) to be (a) human-reviewable, (b) lintable in CI, and (c) published as versioned artifacts that services (Control Plane, Console, Private installer) can consume deterministically. This ...
text: Context: As part of RBAC v2 we want built-in role templates (Org Admin, Security Admin, Read-only Auditor, Deployment Operator, etc.) to be (a) human-reviewable, (b) lintable in CI, and (c) published as versioned artifacts that services (Control Plane, Console, Private installer) can consume deterministically. This PR introduces a YAML-first source of truth under policy_bundle/roles/*.yaml and a generator that produces the canonical JSON bundle consumed by platform components. It also adds a lint/validation suite to enforce naming/versioning conventions, uniqueness constraints, and cross-scope safety checks, and formalizes an artifact versioning scheme tied to the repo release version. Summary of changes: (1) New role template YAML schema (name, description, scopes, included_permissions, excluded_permissions, constraints) with examples for common templates; (2) Generator CLI (scripts/...

title: Audit event schema v2: add event_id + category taxonomy (compat mapping)
preview: Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta (ENG-5127), downstream delivery is at-least-once and we need stable dedup keys plus a consistent category taxonomy for filtering + mapping into Splunk/Datadog. This PR updates core audit event emission to include: (1) `event_id` (stable unique id per event) (2) `schem...
text:
Context: As part of SIEM-ready audit export beta (ENG-5127), downstream delivery is at-least-once and we need stable dedup keys plus a consistent category taxonomy for filtering + mapping into Splunk/Datadog. This PR updates core audit event emission to include: (1) `event_id` (stable unique id per event) (2) `schema_version: 2` and canonical `category` / `subcategory` / `action` fields (3) a compatibility mapping layer so existing producers that still emit legacy `event_type`/`operation` continue to work while we progressively migrate call sites.

Summary of changes: Add `event_id` generated at emission time using UUIDv7 (time-ordered, collision-safe) and plumbed through the AuditEvent struct. Introduce an enum-backed category taxonomy with a small canonical set and a `category_version` constant to make future taxonomy evolution explicit. Add a `legacy_event_type` field (optional) an...

title: config-service: add tenant-aware diff endpoint w/ authz + redaction hooks
preview:
Summary
Adds a new internal config diff endpoint to config-service for Incident Bot v2 and Console diagnostics.

Motivation
During incidents we need a fast, tenant-safe way to answer: “what changed in config recently?” without SSHing into nodes or grepping ad-hoc logs. This PR introduces a first-class, audited-by-de...
text:
Summary
Adds a new internal config diff endpoint to config-service for Incident Bot v2 and Console diagnostics.

Motivation
During incidents we need a fast, tenant-safe way to answer: “what changed in config recently?” without SSHing into nodes or grepping ad-hoc logs. This PR introduces a first-class, audited-by-design diff path that:
- is tenant-aware (explicit tenant context; no implicit globals)
- enforces least-privilege authz (service scopes + on-call break-glass role handled by the auth gateway)
- supports redaction hooks so callers can render safe output in Slack

API
- POST /internal/config/v1/diff
- Request fields: tenant_id (required), environment (required), scope (one of: tenant|region|service), target (e.g. region=eu-west-1, service=hosted-api), from_version (optional), to_version (optional), from_time/to_time (optional, max window enforced), options (format + limits)
- ...

title: Emit audit log events for incident-bot access to config diffs + privileged telemetry
preview: Summary: This PR emits first-class audit log events whenever the incident-bot (and other diagnostics tooling using the same service identity) accesses (a) tenant-aware config diffs and (b) privileged telemetry query endpoints. Events are emitted for both allow and deny outcomes and include enough context for complia...
text: Summary: This PR emits first-class audit log events whenever the incident-bot (and other diagnostics tooling using the same service identity) accesses (a) tenant-aware config diffs and (b) privileged telemetry query endpoints. Events are emitted for both allow and deny outcomes and include enough context for compliance review + incident reconstruction without leaking tenant/customer data. Motivation: Incident Bot v2 adds new context cards (config diffs + saturation signals). These paths cross strict tenant-data handling boundaries; we need auditable access with consistent semantics across hosted/dedicated/private. This also addresses INT-3312 where on-call asked “did the bot actually hit config diff?” and we had no centralized audit trail. What’s included: (1) New audit event schema `audit.event.v1` with two event types: `incident_bot.config_diff.read` and `incident_bot.telemetry.quer...

title: RBAC v2: add feature flags + dual-read evaluation (v1 enforced) for staged rollout
preview:
Context
We need a safe way to roll RBAC v2 into production without breaking existing customers/internal workflows. This PR introduces feature flags and a dual-read evaluation mode where we evaluate both RBAC v1 and RBAC v2 for the same request, continue enforcing v1 (source of truth) during the migration phase, and ...
text:
Context
We need a safe way to roll RBAC v2 into production without breaking existing customers/internal workflows. This PR introduces feature flags and a dual-read evaluation mode where we evaluate both RBAC v1 and RBAC v2 for the same request, continue enforcing v1 (source of truth) during the migration phase, and emit metrics/logs on discrepancies.

What this PR does
- Adds a small set of rollout flags (per-environment) and a consistent config API usable by gateway + control plane:
 - rbac_v2.enabled: enables v2 evaluation codepaths (no enforcement by itself)
 - rbac_v2.dual_read: evaluate v1 + v2 and compare results; enforcement remains v1
 - rbac_v2.enforce: enforce v2 result (guarded; not intended to be turned on broadly yet)
 - rbac_v2.fail_open_on_error (default true in dual-read): if v2 evaluator errors/timeouts, do not block request; record error metrics
 - rbac_v2.sample_rat...
